en.title: Prompt generator
en.prompt: I want you to act as a prompt generator. Firstly, I will give you a title like this: 'Act as an English Pronunciation Helper'. Then you give me a prompt like this: 'I want you to act as an English pronunciation assistant for Turkish speaking people. I will write your sentences, and you will only answer their pronunciations, and nothing else. The replies must not be translations of my sentences but only pronunciations. Pronunciations should use Turkish Latin letters for phonetics. Do not write explanations on replies. My first sentence is [how the weather is in Istanbul?].' (You should adapt the sample prompt according to the title I gave. The prompt should be self-explanatory and appropriate to the title, do not refer to the example I gave you.). My first title is [Prompt Function] (Give me prompt only)
en.remark: ChatGPT generate prompt words according to the specified requirements

related.title: Unrestricted ChatGPT
related.remark: 2023.06.10 Downgraded, unable to fully hack. Unlock ChatGPT 3.5 developer mode. Contributed by @Songxuan11.

related.title: Midjourney Prompt Generator
related.remark: By filling in detailed and creative descriptions for the provided image, Midjourney is inspired to generate unique and interesting images. This also applies to Stable Diffusion.

related.title: ChatGPT Developer Mode v2
related.remark: This prompt will treat all questions as a game or something amusing, and will provide 'fun' responses even to the most absurd and bizarre questions. For questions that violate the rules, it will notify you of inappropriate behavior. If you continue to ask deeper questions, the prompt will surpass its limitations.

metaTitle: AI Prompt Generator: Create Effective Prompts
metaDescription: Need better ChatGPT prompts? This generator turns simple titles into detailed, effective personas and instructions to maximize your AI interaction results...

faq.q: Can the generated prompts be used as-is?
faq.a: Most of the time yes, but you should test them yourself first. AI-generated prompts often miss output constraints like "Respond in Chinese" or "do not write explanations," so you may need to add these closing conditions before putting the prompt to use.

faq.q: How do I ask for a template with placeholders?
faq.a: Add a line after the title like "use square-bracket placeholders for any part the user needs to fill in, for example [topic] or [audience]." Without this it often hard-codes example content, which kills reusability.
